steps.title: Find the clause boundary
steps.content: The sentence links two independent clauses: the guide grew in scope and influence, and its modest French eateries were replaced with esteemed restaurants from around the world. Independent clauses joined with and need a comma before the conjunction.

steps.title: Select the complete join
steps.content: Choice A leaves the clauses run together with no punctuation or conjunction. Choice B uses only a comma, creating a comma splice. Choice D has the conjunction but lacks the comma needed before it. The comma plus and correctly joins the two clauses. The answer is C.
